chapter_title.ar: باب تَفْضِيلِ الْقَرَابَةِ فِي الزَّكَاةِ وَ مَنْ لَا يَجُوزُ مِنْهُمْ أَنْ يُعْطَوْا مِنَ الزَّكَاةِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 33 – Preferring of the near relatives regarding the Zakāt, and the ones who are not allowed from them that they should be given from the Zakāt

text: سَأَلْتُ الصَّادِقَ ( عليه السلام ) أَدْفَعُ عُشْرَ مَالِي إِلَى وُلْدِ ابْنَتِي قَالَ نَعَمْ لَا بَأْسَ .

en.hubeali: Ahmad Bin Idrees, and someone else, from Muhammad Bin Ahmad, from one of our companions, from Muhammad Bin Jazzaki who said,
‘I asked Al-Sadiq<sup>asws</sup>, ‘Can I hand over the tax (10%) of my wealth to the children of my daughter?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Yes, and there is no problem’.
en.sarwar: 10. Ahmad ibn Idris and others have narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from certain persons of our people from Muhammad ibn Jazzak who has said the following: “I once asked al-Sadiq, abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, ‘Can I give one out of ten of my asset to the children of my daughter? He (the Imam) said, ‘Yes, it is not harmful.’”
